title: Simulation: add enter_rx_mode() with callback
description:
Add non-blocking RX mode entry to python/src/lichen/sim/simulation.py:

```python
def enter_rx_mode(
    self,
    node_id: str,
    timeout_us: int,
    on_packet: Callable[[bytes, int, int], None],  # payload, rssi, snr
    on_timeout: Callable[[], None],
) -> None:
    """Enter RX mode with callbacks (non-blocking).
    
    Puts node in RX_WAIT state so BARRIER_SYNC can advance time.
    Calls on_packet(payload, rssi, snr) when a packet arrives.
    Calls on_timeout() if timeout_us expires with no packet.
    
    Only one callback fires, then node exits RX_WAIT.
    """
    node = self._get_node(node_id)
    node.state = NodeState.RX_WAIT
    node.rx_callbacks = (on_packet, on_timeout)  # store for event processing
    
    # Queue timeout event
    self._event_queue.push(RxTimeoutEvent(
        time_us=self._current_time_us + timeout_us,
        node_id=node_id,
    ))

def exit_rx_mode(self, node_id: str) -> None:
    """Exit RX mode, cancel pending timeout."""
    node = self._get_node(node_id)
    node.state = NodeState.IDLE
    node.rx_callbacks = None
    # Remove pending RxTimeoutEvent for this node
```

Modify event processing to call the callbacks when RxTimeoutEvent fires or when a packet is delivered to the node.
acceptance_criteria: enter_rx_mode() puts node in RX_WAIT; callbacks fire on packet/timeout; unit test confirms BARRIER_SYNC advances time
close_reason: Added enter_rx_mode/exit_rx_mode to Simulation with callbacks for packet/timeout events
